questions.question: The ancient Egyptians built the Great Pyramid using $2x \cdot 3y$ stones. If they wanted to factor out the greatest common factor, what would it be?
The ancient Greek city of Babylon had $12x + 16$ olive trees. What is the greatest common factor of this expression?
The Mayan civilization used $9x^2 + 27x$ to calculate the area of their temples. What is the factored form of this expression?
The ancient Chinese used $24x - 30$ to measure the length of the Great Wall. What is the greatest common factor of this expression?
The Inca Empire used $15x + 25$ to calculate the amount of gold they had. What is the greatest common factor of this expression?
The ancient Indians used $8x^2 - 24x$ to calculate the area of their crops. What is the factored form of this expression?
The ancient Egyptians used $6x + 18$ to measure the length of their pyramids. What is the greatest common factor of this expression?
The ancient Greeks used $20x - 12$ to calculate the amount of olive oil they produced. What is the greatest common factor of this expression?
The ancient Mesopotamians used $12x^2 + 36x$ to calculate the area of their cities. Factor out the greatest common factor and simplify the expression. Show your work.
The ancient Aztecs used $15x + 30$ to calculate the amount of food they had. Factor out the greatest common factor and simplify the expression. Explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Make sure students show their work for the free response questions.
Tip 2: Encourage students to use different methods to factor out the greatest common factor.
Tip 3: Remind students to check their answers for accuracy.
